Q: A 54-year-old man with a history of hypertension presents to the emergency department 3 hours after sudden onset of left-sided weakness, gaze deviation, and decreased level of alertness. A: No, intravenous glibenclamide should not be initiated because it is an investigational therapy that failed to show a clinical benefit in a phase III trial for patients with large hemispheric infarction and is not recommended in current acute stroke guidelines.
